text: Databases help us store and organize information. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases use tables with rows and columns, like spreadsheets, making them great for structured data with clear relationships. To retrieve data, you write queries using SQL, a special language. NoSQL databases are more flexible, handling unstructured data like documents or graphs. Data modeling is the process of designing how data is stored, whether in tables or other formats. Indexes are tools that speed up queries by acting like book indexes, helping you find data quickly without checking every row. Choosing the right type depends on your project's needs.
